prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) were computed using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges used the Mulliken scheme (GFN2-xTB native). The N±1 states used charge +1/mult 2 (cation) and charge −1/mult 2 (anion). Geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz without re-optimization.

The headline value reported (0.22885456) is the f⁺ index on the Fe atom (atom 11, 1-based), which is both the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic site. Fe f⁻ = 0.25938191, Fe f⁰ = 0.24411824, Fe dual descriptor = −0.030527354. All 10 cyclopentadienyl C atoms carry f⁺ ≈ 0.01364, and H atoms carry f⁺ ≈ 0.06348, reflecting the molecule's high D₅h/D₅d symmetry.

The dual descriptor on Fe is negative (−0.0305), classifying it as the nucleophilic center (susceptible to electrophilic attack), consistent with its d-electron-rich coordination environment. All C and H atoms have positive dual descriptors (~+0.0015–0.0016), marking them as locally electrophilic.

Integrity: trustworthy = true. sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (charge conservation confirmed). No warnings reported. Plot saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-175020_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
